seoTitle: How to Convert a Canva Design to PDF
metaDescription: Learn how to convert your Canva design to PDF for sharing, printing, or presenting, with tips to keep quality high.
intro: Exporting a Canva design as a PDF is one of the most common final steps, whether you're sending a document to a client, printing a report, or archiving a presentation. This guide covers the export options and how to choose the right one.

steps.heading: Finish editing your design
steps.body: Make sure all pages and elements are finalized before exporting.

steps.heading: Open the download menu
steps.body: Click the Share or Download button in the top-right corner of the editor.

steps.heading: Select PDF as the file type
steps.body: Choose between PDF Standard, for general sharing, or PDF Print, for higher-quality printed output.

steps.heading: Choose which pages to include
steps.body: If your design has multiple pages, select all pages or just the ones you need in the export.

steps.heading: Download and review
steps.body: Save the file and open it to confirm the layout and text look correct before sending it out.

faq.question: What's the difference between PDF Standard and PDF Print?
faq.answer: PDF Standard is optimized for smaller file sizes and screen sharing, while PDF Print produces higher resolution suited for physical printing.

faq.question: Can I convert a multi-page Canva design to a single PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, all pages are combined automatically into one PDF file during export.

faq.question: Is exporting to PDF free in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, both PDF export options are available without a Canva Pro subscription.

faq.question: Will my fonts stay the same in the PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva embeds fonts in the exported PDF so text appears exactly as designed.
